tests.scenario: User accesses basic mental health tracking features with free account
steps: Create a new free account
Log in to the system with the free account credentials
Navigate to the mental health tracking features section
tests.expected_result: The user can access and view basic mental health tracking features.

tests.scenario: User attempts to access premium features with free account
steps: Create a new free account
Log in to the system with the free account credentials
Navigate to a premium mental health tracking feature section
tests.expected_result: The system restricts access to premium features and displays a message indicating that the feature is not available in the free version.

tests.scenario: User views account settings and sees paid subscription options
steps: Log in to the system with a free version account
Navigate to the account settings page
Verify that the paid subscription options are displayed
Verify that the benefits of each paid subscription option are listed
tests.expected_result: The system displays the available paid subscription options and their benefits.

tests.scenario: User views account settings with no active account
steps: Attempt to navigate to the account settings page without logging in
Verify that the system prompts for login credentials
tests.expected_result: The system requires the user to log in before displaying account settings.

tests.scenario: User logs a mood and views mood history
steps: Log in to the system
Navigate to the mood tracking feature
Select a mood from the available options
Add a note to the mood log
Submit the mood log
View the mood history
tests.expected_result: The system displays a visual representation of the user's mood history, including the recently logged mood and note.

tests.scenario: User attempts to view mood history without logging a mood
steps: Log in to the system
Navigate to the mood tracking feature
View the mood history without logging a mood
tests.expected_result: The system displays an empty or default mood history, with a message indicating that no mood logs have been recorded yet.

tests.scenario: User successfully accesses and saves a reflection
steps: Access the reflection prompts library
Select a reflection prompt
Write a reflection in the text input field
Save the reflection
tests.expected_result: The reflection is saved to the user's journal

tests.scenario: User attempts to save an empty reflection
steps: Access the reflection prompts library
Select a reflection prompt
Save the reflection without writing anything
tests.expected_result: The system prevents the reflection from being saved and displays an error message

tests.scenario: User views emotional progress graph successfully
steps: Log in to the system with valid credentials
Navigate to the tracking section
Select a time period (e.g., week, month)
Verify the system displays a graphical representation of emotions and feelings
tests.expected_result: The system displays a clear and accurate graphical representation of the user's emotions and feelings over the selected time period.

tests.scenario: User views emotional progress graph with no logged emotions
steps: Log in to the system with valid credentials
Navigate to the tracking section
Select a time period with no logged emotions (e.g., a new user with no entries)
Verify the system's response
tests.expected_result: The system displays a message indicating no data is available for the selected time period or displays an empty graph with a corresponding message.

tests.scenario: User receives personalized recommendations after tracking emotions for a week
steps: Log in to the application
Navigate to the emotion tracking section
Track emotions and feelings for at least 7 days
Navigate to the insights section
tests.expected_result: The system displays 3-5 personalized recommendations for managing mental health.

tests.scenario: User does not receive recommendations if they haven't tracked emotions for a week
steps: Log in to the application
Navigate to the emotion tracking section
Track emotions and feelings for less than 7 days
Navigate to the insights section
tests.expected_result: The system does not display any personalized recommendations.

tests.scenario: User can access a month's emotion summary
steps: Launch the app and navigate to the free version
Track emotions daily for 30 days
Navigate to the history section
tests.expected_result: The system displays a summary of emotions for the past month.

tests.scenario: User cannot access summary with incomplete tracking
steps: Launch the app and navigate to the free version
Track emotions for 29 days
Navigate to the history section
tests.expected_result: The system does not display a summary of emotions for the past month and provides a message indicating incomplete tracking.

tests.scenario: User views detailed reports with premium subscription
steps: Subscribe to the premium version of the service
Track emotions for over a year
Navigate to the reports section
Select the monthly and yearly reports options
tests.expected_result: The system displays detailed monthly and yearly reports of the user's mental health trends.

tests.scenario: User attempts to view reports without premium subscription
steps: Track emotions for over a year without subscribing to premium
Navigate to the reports section
Select the monthly and yearly reports options
tests.expected_result: The system displays an error message or a prompt to subscribe to the premium version to access the reports.

tests.scenario: Successful data restoration after update
steps: Create a new user account and add some data (e.g., notes, files, settings)
Simulate an app update
Log out of the app
Log back in to the app
tests.expected_result: The user's data is accurately restored, and all information is present and unchanged.

tests.scenario: Data restoration failure due to corrupted data
steps: Create a new user account and add some data (e.g., notes, files, settings)
Corrupt the user's data storage (simulate data corruption)
Simulate an app update
Log out of the app
Log back in to the app
tests.expected_result: The app displays an error message indicating that data restoration failed, and the user is prompted to reset their data.

tests.scenario: User is not asked a repeated question
steps: Log in as a user
Answer a question in a given context
Navigate away and return to the same context
Verify the question is not asked again
tests.expected_result: The system does not ask the user the same question again.

tests.scenario: User is asked a question if context changes
steps: Log in as a user
Answer a question in a given context
Change the context
Return to the original context
Verify the question is asked again
tests.expected_result: The system asks the user the question again because the context has changed.
